Sprint 7 Review Meeting - March 27, 2026
Attendees: Alex, Jordan, Sam, Taylor, Casey

Meeting Started: 2:00 PM
Location: Conference Room B / Virtual

AGENDA:
1. Sprint 7 accomplishments review
2. Demo of completed features
3. Retrospective discussion
4. Planning for Sprint 8

DISCUSSION POINTS:

Feature Completion Status:
- User authentication module: COMPLETED (Alex)
- Payment gateway integration: COMPLETED (Jordan)
- Admin dashboard v1: 90% complete, needs polish (Sam)
- Mobile responsive layouts: COMPLETED (Taylor)
- API rate limiting: COMPLETED (Casey)

Demo Highlights:
- Auth flow working smoothly, 2FA implemented
- Payment processing tested with Stripe sandbox
- Dashboard needs UX improvements per user feedback
- Mobile views tested on iOS and Android devices
- Rate limiting prevents abuse, logs properly

KEY DECISIONS:

1. API Versioning Strategy
   - Decision: Adopt semantic versioning for API
   - Current version will be v2.3.0
   - Breaking changes require major version bump
   - Deprecation notices must be given 3 months in advance
   - Rationale: Industry standard, clear communication with clients

2. Documentation Priority
   - All new features must have docs before merge
   - API reference to be auto-generated from OpenAPI spec
   - User guides to be written by feature owners
   - Weekly doc review sessions scheduled

3. Testing Standards
   - Minimum 80% code coverage for new code
   - Integration tests required for API endpoints
   - E2E tests for critical user flows
   - Performance testing for high-traffic endpoints

4. Sprint 8 Focus Areas
   - Complete admin dashboard polish
   - Implement user profile management
   - Add notification system
   - Improve API documentation
   - Security audit preparation

ACTION ITEMS:

1. Sam: Complete admin dashboard UX improvements by Wed
2. Taylor: Create API v2.3.0 changelog by Thu
3. Casey: Set up automated doc generation pipeline
4. Alex: Schedule security audit with external firm
5. Jordan: Document payment integration patterns
6. All: Update personal task boards for Sprint 8

RETROSPECTIVE FEEDBACK:

What Went Well:
- Team collaboration was excellent
- Features delivered on time
- Code reviews were thorough
- Communication improved significantly

What Could Improve:
- Need better estimation for UI tasks
- Documentation lagging behind development
- More frequent stakeholder updates needed
- Test coverage still below target in some areas

What to Try Next Sprint:
- Daily async standups in Slack
- Pair programming for complex features
- Documentation sprints on Fridays
- Bi-weekly stakeholder demos

METRICS:
- Velocity: 42 story points (target: 40)
- Bugs found: 8 (all resolved)
- Customer satisfaction: 4.2/5.0
- Team morale: High

Meeting Ended: 3:45 PM

Next Sprint Planning: March 30, 9:00 AM

arguments.content:
# Sprint 7 — Sprint Review Conclusions

Date: **2026-03-27**  
Time: **2:00 PM – 3:45 PM**  
Location: **Conference Room B / Virtual**  
Sprint Reviewed: **Sprint 7**  

Attendees: **Alex, Jordan, Sam, Taylor, Casey**

---

## 1) Meeting Overview

The team reviewed Sprint 7 accomplishments, walked through demos of completed work, discussed retrospective feedback (what went well / what to improve / experiments for next sprint), and aligned on focus areas for Sprint 8.

### Sprint 7 Delivery Summary
- **User authentication module** — Completed (Owner: Alex)
- **Payment gateway integration** — Completed (Owner: Jordan)
- **Admin dashboard v1** — ~90% complete; requires UX polish (Owner: Sam)
- **Mobile responsive layouts** — Completed (Owner: Taylor)
- **API rate limiting** — Completed (Owner: Casey)

### Demo Highlights
- Authentication flow validated; **2FA implemented**.
- Payment processing tested successfully with **Stripe sandbox**.
- Admin dashboard surfaced **UX improvement needs** based on user feedback.
- Mobile views tested on **iOS and Android**.
- Rate limiting validated for abuse prevention; **logging confirmed**.

---

## 2) Key Decisions

### 2.1 API Versioning Strategy
- **Decision:** Adopt **semantic versioning** for the API.
- **Current version:** `v2.3.0`.
- **Policy:**
  - Breaking changes require a **major** version bump.
  - Deprecation notices must be provided **3 months in advance**.
- **Rationale:** Aligns with industry standard and improves clarity for client communications.

### 2.2 Documentation Priority & Ownership
- **Decision:** Documentation is a merge prerequisite for new features.
- **Implementation:**
  - API reference will be **auto-generated from the OpenAPI spec**.
  - User guides will be written by **feature owners**.
  - Weekly **documentation review sessions** will be scheduled.

### 2.3 Testing Standards
- **Decision:** Establish minimum quality gates for new work.
- **Standards:**
  - Minimum **80% code coverage** for new code.
  - **Integration tests** required for API endpoints.
  - **E2E tests** for critical user flows.
  - **Performance testing** for high-traffic endpoints.

### 2.4 Sprint 8 Focus Areas
- **Decision:** Sprint 8 will prioritize:
  - Completing **admin dashboard polish**
  - Implementing **user profile management**
  - Adding a **notification system**
  - Improving **API documentation**
  - Preparing for a **security audit**

---

## 3) Action Items

| # | Owner | Action | Due |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | Sam | Complete admin dashboard UX improvements | Wed |
| 2 | Taylor | Create `v2.3.0` API changelog | Thu |
| 3 | Casey | Set up automated documentation generation pipeline | TBD |
| 4 | Alex | Schedule security audit with external firm | TBD |
| 5 | Jordan | Document payment integration patterns | TBD |
| 6 | All | Update personal task boards for Sprint 8 | ASAP |

---

## Appendix: Retrospective Notes (Captured)

### What Went Well
- Team collaboration was excellent
- Features delivered on time
- Code reviews were thorough
- Communication improved significantly

### What Could Improve
- Better estimation needed for UI tasks
- Documentation lagging behind development
- More frequent stakeholder updates needed
- Test coverage below target in some areas

### What to Try Next Sprint
- Daily async standups in Slack
- Pair programming for complex features
- Documentation sprints on Fridays
- Bi-weekly stakeholder demos

### Metrics
- Velocity: **42 story points** (target: 40)
- Bugs found: **8** (all resolved)
- Customer satisfaction: **4.2 / 5.0**
- Team morale: **High**

---

Next Sprint Planning: **2026-03-30, 9:00 AM**
